response:
# Top 3 changes to `src/billing.ts`

## 1. Break up `generateInvoice` (crime_00002, knocks down crime_00016)

204 lines spanning L50–253 — 3.4× the domain threshold and the single highest-severity finding in the file (severity 0.9). It's also the reason the file as a whole trips God File at 310 lines; carve cohesive sections out of `generateInvoice` and the file shrinks below the 300-line line by construction. Extract along responsibility seams (line-item assembly, tax/proration, persistence, notification) so an agent editing one section can't silently break the others.

## 2. Encode the L39 comment rule and rename `calculateRefundPreview` (crime_00017 + crime_00013)

These are the same bug wearing two hats. The comment at L39 — *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves"* — is a policy the surrounding code doesn't visibly enforce, and the function it precedes (`calculateRefundPreview`, L40–44) lies about itself: a "preview" / "calculate" name that actually calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail`. An agent reads the name, assumes pure, and ships an edit that double-charges audit rows or fires a second email. Fix by (a) extracting the role/plan check into a named guard (`assertCanRefundEnterprise(actor, plan)`) so the rule lives in code, and (b) splitting the function into `buildRefundPreview` (pure) + `commitRefund` (the audit + email side effects). This also gives the still-empty tests in `src/billing.test.ts` (crime_00011) something real to assert against.

## 3. Inject a clock for the 7 `Date` calls in billing (crime_00009)

Seven direct `Date.now()` / `new Date()` calls across L57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273. Invoice generation that reads wall-time is what's keeping the existing tests weak — "renders the enterprise plan" asserts nothing (crime_00011) and "creates an invoice" only uses a weak matcher (crime_00030) because there's no way to pin a stable `issuedAt`/`dueAt` without monkey-patching globals. Thread a `clock: () => Date` (or `now: () => number`) parameter through `generateInvoice` and its new helpers from step 1; default it to `Date.now` at the CLI/API boundary. Once invoice timestamps are deterministic, the tests can assert exact totals, due dates, and proration without flake — and the shared `src/date.ts` cluster (crime_00003, _00004, _00010, _00014) becomes the next obvious thing to clean up using the same clock seam.
